items.content: The humans were left far behind as smartphone maker Honor's humanoid robot shattered the men's world record in China.

items.content: Humanoid robots raced alongside humans in a half-marathon in Beijing.

items.title: 'New era': Humanoid robot outruns humans in Beijing half-marathon, beats world record
items.standfirst: A humanoid robot on Sunday won a half-marathon for robots in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record time and showcasing China's technological advances.
items.content: A humanoid robot on Sunday won a half-marathon for robots in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record time and showcasing China's technological advances.

items.title: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon

items.content: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

items.title: In a race between humans and robots, machines take the victory in a sign of advancements

items.content: BEIJING — It was not even close as a bright red Chinese humanoid named “Lightning” lived up to its name in a half-marathon pitting humans against robots, smoking its competition on Sunday

items.content: Tehran will keep the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed until Washington ends the blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: Middle East war live: Strait of Hormuz stays shut as Iran reports 'progress' in US talks
items.standfirst: Iran’s parliamentary speaker said Saturday night that talks with the United States to end the war had made progress but remained far from an agreement, as Tehran said the crucial Strait of Hormuz would remain closed until Washington lifts its naval blockade on Iranian ports. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire expires this week with no deal in sight
items.standfirst: After briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz, Iran has again closed the vital economic waterway, saying it will restrict ships from passing through as long as the U.S. continues its blockade.
items.content: After briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz, Iran has again closed the vital economic waterway, saying it will restrict ships from passing through as long as the U.S. continues its blockade.(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)

items.title: Trump shifts message on Iran war
items.standfirst: President Trump said “very good conversations” are taking place with Iran after warning the U.S. may start “dropping bombs again” if no deal is met by next week when the ceasefire with Iran expires. It comes as Iran is declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed in response to the U.S. continuing its blockade of Iranian ports. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin reports.
items.content: President Trump said “very good conversations” are taking place with Iran after warning the U.S. may start “dropping bombs again” if no deal is met by next week when the ceasefire with Iran expires. It comes as Iran is declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed in response to the U.S. continuing its blockade of Iranian ports. NBC News’ Julie Tsikrin reports.

clusters.title: Bulgaria votes as pro-Russian former president leads in the polls
clusters.neutral_headline: Bulgaria votes as pro-Russian former president leads in the polls
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Bulgaria votes as pro-Russian former president leads in the polls
items.standfirst: Eighth election in five years follows government collapse in December, with stability and cost of living among key issuesBulgarians are voting in the eighth parliamentary election in five years, with the clear frontrunner, the pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev, promising to stamp out corruption and end a succession of weak, short-lived governments.Radev, a Eurosceptic former fighter pilot who has opposed military support for Ukraine, stepped down from the presidency in January to run in the election, which comes after mass demonstrations forced out the previous government in December. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Middle East: Hezbollah blamed after French peacekeeper killed in Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: Middle East: Hezbollah blamed after French peacekeeper killed in Lebanon
items.standfirst: A French peacekeeper serving with United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon was killed and three others wounded in an ambush in southern Lebanon, with Emmanuel Macron blaming Hezbollah, which denies responsibility.
items.content: A French peacekeeper serving with United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon was killed and three others wounded in an ambush in southern Lebanon, with Emmanuel Macron blaming Hezbollah, which denies responsibility.

items.title: What is UNIFIL? the UN mission in southern Lebanon explained
items.standfirst: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) has patrolled southern Lebanon along the border with Israel since 1978, acting as a buffer force following Israel’s invasion of the region. Expanded after the 2006 war between Israel and Hezbollah, the mission now monitors the ceasefire and supports the Lebanese army, with more than 7,500 peacekeepers deployed—though it has no mandate to intervene militarily.
items.content: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) has patrolled southern Lebanon along the border with Israel since 1978, acting as a buffer force following Israel’s invasion of the region. Expanded after the 2006 war between Israel and Hezbollah, the mission now monitors the ceasefire and supports the Lebanese army, with more than 7,500 peacekeepers deployed—though it has no mandate to intervene militarily.

items.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.
items.content: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

items.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah was responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah was responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.

items.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
items.standfirst: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.
items.content: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.

items.content: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to New York was diverted to Pittsburgh Saturday afternoon. Passengers were forced to evacuate via emergency slides.

items.title: United plane makes emergency landing in Pittsburgh over ‘possible security issue’
items.standfirst: Passengers on flight from Chicago to New York exited via slides as FBI, bomb technicians and K-9 unit responded Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxA United Airlines flight bound for New York City was forced to land and evacuate in Pittsburgh on Saturday morning after crew members reported a “possible security issue”, a spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said.United flight 2092 took off from Chicago O’Hare airport and had been en route to LaGuardia airport in New York. The plane diverted to Pittsburgh international airport as a result of the reported threat. Continue reading...

The plane diverted to Pittsburgh international airport as a result of the reported threat. Continue reading...

items.title: Oil price drops below $90 a barrel after Iran says strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: Gas price also falls and markets rise amid hopes Donald Trump will succeed in reaching deal with TehranOil and gas prices fell sharply on Friday after Iran said the strait of Hormuz was open to commercial shipping, potentially clearing the way for tankers holding millions of barrels of oil and gas to reach the global market.Iran’s foreign minister said vessels would be free to transit the strait of Hormuz for the duration of the 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, which was struck on Thursday.
